allies.rationale_text: Premortem complements regret-theory by turning prospective hindsight into a structured review of what could go wrong before commitment.
allies.source_quote: RMF functions as an emotionally driven premortem

allies.rationale_text: Optionality strengthens regret-theory by checking whether action can be made more reversible before anticipated regret hardens the choice.
allies.source_quote: If the decision is reversible, there is nothing to lose and only information and speed to gain

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias is countered by regret-theory when the framework forces the decision maker to consider the regretted path of inaction instead of only the comfortable default story.
antagonists.source_quote: helps fight the confirmation bias that typically favors the comfortable status quo

structured_tensions.tension_text: Regret-theory conflicts with probabilistic-thinking when anticipated regret distorts probability weighting toward vivid worst-case scenarios.
structured_tensions.source_quote: regret-theory conflicts with probabilistic-thinking (probabilistic thinking) when anticipated regret distorts probability weighting toward vivid worst-case scenarios.

curation_notes.summary: The retained relation surface stays narrow around prospective hindsight, reversibility, and the explicit conflict with comfort-preserving confirmation-bias.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader expected-utility or satisficing expansion because the current vocabulary already supports a cleaner decision-pressure surface.
open_questions: The source also gestures toward outside-view and data discipline, but the retained set favors the clearest current-vocabulary ids.
